== Notes for [[Item:Q2389|Q2389]] ==

{geni:about_me} HE WAS MARRIED TO GORI, DAUGHTER OF CHANDRAVANSHI KING MATINAAR.
<p>[https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Mandhatri#cite_note-5 Mandhatri or Mandhata ](Sanskrit: मान्धातृ, Māndhātṛ), in Hindu mythology, was an Ikshvaku dynasty king and son of Yuvanashva.[1] The hymn 134 of the tenth mandala of the Rigveda is attributed to him.[2] He married Bindumati Chaitrarathi, daughter of Yadava king Shashabindu and granddaughter of Chitraratha.[3] According to the Puranas, he had three sons, Purukutsa, Ambarisha and Muchukunda.[4] According to the Mahabharata, he was a son of the Suryavansha king Yuvanshva.[5]
<p>http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/index.html</p>
<p>http://www.gloriousindia.com/history/dynasties/ikshvaku/yuvanashva_ii.html He was married to Gori, daughter of the Chandravanshi king Matinaar</p>

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
6707A5D5504AE1EFBBFC9A22EF79916B2985


[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
